description: Exp104c: Better handling of structural tokens (. and ') with increased model capacity

model.note: Increased capacity vs exp104b (emb 192→256, hidden 384→512) for better contextual disambiguation of structural tokens

training.note: Larger batch size (64→96) for better gradient stability with rare tokens

experiment.name: exp104c_structural_tokens
experiment.purpose: Handle . ↔ ˈ ↔ <PAD> confusions with increased model capacity + better gradient flow
compared_with: exp104b: 0.51% PER (0.90% Class D) - baseline with structural token issues
exp1: 0.64% PER (1.02% Class D) - pure baseline without DA Loss
experiment.hypothesis: Larger LSTM hidden state allows better contextual understanding of structural tokens (syllable markers, stress markers), reducing confusions. Target: 0.48-0.50% PER with 0.75-0.80% Class D.
key_changes: Same DA Loss (λ=0.20), same architecture (BiLSTM 2 layers)
